title: podman can't use the netavark ipvlan driver
body:
### Issue Description

netavark 1.5 added support for ipvlan networks but podman 4.4.1 can't create them yet.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman network create --driver ipvlan`


### Describe the results you received

`Error: unsupported driver ipvlan: invalid argument`

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should create the network. For now we can edit the netavark .json  definition manually.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 4.4.1, netavark 1.5
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

fedora coreos

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I wouldn't call this a bug. It just needs someone implementing and pushing the change through to podman, see https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1266
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
